The input is a JSON with a key "xml_string" containing an XML string. We must output a JSON with fixed_xml, number_of_corrections, and explanation.

The XML has several issues: unclosed tags, mismatched tags, and incorrect tags. For example, there is a closing tag </BIBL> but the opening is <BIBL> in many places. Also, <PRIZE> vs <PRICE> inconsistency. Also nested <BIBL> within <BIBL> (e.g., inside "Ejusd. Politicorum Libri" there are nested <BIBL> tags). Also <BIBL> without closing? Actually, the structure: there is a single <ITEM> containing many <BIBL> elements. But inside the third BIBL (Ejusd. Politicorum Libri), there are multiple inner <BIBL> tags, which is invalid because BIBL elements should not be directly nested like that; they should be siblings. Also the tag <VOLUME> appears, and <PLACE>, <YEAR>, etc. Also the text has some unusual entities like \u221a\u2020 etc. But those are probably encoding issues from the original. We should not change those.

We need to restructure the XML to make it well-formed. The original seems to treat each book entry as a separate <BIBL>. But the nesting inside the "Ejusd. Politicorum Libri" entry: it has multiple inner <BIBL> tags, each with a title and format and prize. That might be intended as separate works by the same author. However, XML nesting is not appropriate; they should be separate sibling <BIBL> elements. Also note that the first BIBL has an author Justi Lipsii, then several "Ejusd." (same). So it seems the author is repeated. The structure should be: <ITEM> containing multiple <BIBL> elements, each with AUTHOR, TITLE, FORMAT, PRIZE, etc. Where there are multiple works by the same author, they are separate BIBL elements.

Looking at the nested BIBLs: inside "Ejusd. Politicorum Libri" there are three inner BIBLs: Monita 8e Exempla politica, De Magnirudine Romana, De Constantia. Then after that, another outer BIBL for "Ejusd. de Militia Romana" etc. So the inner BIBLs should be promoted to top-level siblings. Also note that the first BIBL ends with </BIBL> after the prize, then next BIBL starts. But the third BIBL (Ejusd. Politicorum Libri) has no closing </BIBL> until after the inner BIBLs? Let's parse:

Original string:

... <BIBL><AUTHOR>Justi Lipsii</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Epistolarum Centuriae complet</TITLE>, in <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> apud Plautin. [symbol] <PRIZE>1. Gulden 36. xr.</PRIZE></BIBL>
<BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Politicorum Libri</TITLE>. <BIBL><TITLE>Monita</TITLE> <VOLUME>8e Exempla politica</VOLUME>.</BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>De Magnirudine Romana</TITLE>.</BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>De Constantia</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> ibid. [symbol] <PRIZE>fl. 1. xr. 36.</PRIZE></BIBL>
<BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>de Militia Romana cum An. fig.</TITLE> <FORMAT>4to</FORMAT>-ibid. [symbol] <PRIZE>fl. 1. xr. 30.</PRIZE></BIBL>
...

So the second BIBL (Ejusd. Politicorum Libri) contains three nested BIBL elements. The nesting is invalid because we have <BIBL> inside <BIBL>. To fix, we should separate them: treat each work as its own BIBL. Also note that the author for the nested ones is "Ejusd." (the same). So we can repeat the author for each. Also there is a <VOLUME> tag inside that first inner BIBL.

Also note that there is a spelling error: "PRIZE" vs "PRICE" - there is one instance of <PRICE>48. xr.</PRICE> instead of <PRIZE>. We should standardize to <PRIZE> as that is the most common.

Also missing closing tags? Let's check the overall structure: The string starts with <ITEM> ... and ends with </ITEM>. That seems fine. But we need to ensure all tags are properly closed. In the original, there are many unclosed tags? Actually, the inner BIBLs: after the third inner BIBL (De Constantia) there is a </BIBL> which closes that inner BIBL, but then the outer BIBL (Politicorum Libri) is not closed until later? Let's see: after the inner BIBLs, the next BIBL starts with <BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>de Militia Romana ... So the outer BIBL enclosing Politicorum Libri is never closed? Actually, the nesting suggests that the outer BIBL for Politicorum Libri includes those inner BIBLs. The closing </BIBL> after the inner ones might be intended to close the outer? But there is no explicit closing for the outer. The text after the inner BIBLs is: </BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> ... So that </BIBL> might be closing the outer BIBL? But then the next <BIBL> starts. However, the inner BIBLs also have their own </BIBL> tags. So the parsing is ambiguous.

Better approach: Since we are to fix the XML, we should restructure it to be a flat list of BIBL elements inside ITEM. Each BIBL should have the author repeated if from the same author. Also ensure each BIBL has proper closing tags. Also note that some BIBL elements have missing FORMAT or PRIZE.

Let's count the original BIBL elements. I'll parse the string into a list of book entries. The original has many [symbol] entities which are likely Unicode characters. We keep them.

I'll rewrite the XML as a flat structure. Also note that there is a <BIBL> with no AUTHOR? For example: <BIBL><TITLE>Delicia Urbis Bernae</TITLE>... That's fine.

Also note that inside the nested BIBLs, the first inner BIBL has <TITLE>Monita</TITLE> and <VOLUME>8e Exempla politica</VOLUME>. There is no FORMAT or PRIZE for that one? Actually it ends with a dot and then </BIBL>. So it might be part of a multi-volume work. But we treat it as a separate BIBL entry with title and volume only? That seems incomplete. Perhaps the original intent was to list sub-works under a main title. But to make valid XML, we need to decide. I think we should keep them as separate BIBL elements, but if they lack FORMAT or PRIZE, they might be considered incomplete. But we can still have them.

Another issue: There is a tag <PRIZE> inside the inner BIBL for De Constantia, that's fine.

Also note that there is a <YEAR> tag in some BIBLs.

Now, I'll manually reconstruct the XML.

First, let's list all the book entries as they appear, breaking at each <BIBL> start. I'll treat the text as a stream.

Original (approximate):

1. Justi Lipsii Epistolarum ... 4to. apud Plautin. [symbol] 1 Gulden 36 xr. (has closing </BIBL>)

2. Ejusd. Politicorum Libri. (then three inner BIBLs: Monita, De Magnirudine Romana, De Constantia with format and prize). After that, the text has </BIBL> which likely closes this outer BIBL? Actually, the inner BIBLs each have closing </BIBL>? The inner ones: <BIBL><TITLE>Monita</TITLE> <VOLUME>8e Exempla politica</VOLUME>.</BIBL> That ends with </BIBL>. Then <BIBL><TITLE>De Magnirudine Romana</TITLE>.</BIBL> Then <BIBL><TITLE>De Constantia</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> ibid. [symbol] <PRIZE>fl. 1. xr. 36.</PRIZE></BIBL>. So after these three inner BIBLs, the outer BIBL for Politicorum Libri is not closed? But then immediately after the third inner BIBL's </BIBL>, there is another <BIBL> for de Militia Romana. So the outer BIBL might be implicitly closed by the next <BIBL>? However, to be well-formed, each opening tag must have a closing tag. So the outer BIBL that started before Politicorum Libri must be closed somewhere. The only </BIBL> after the inner ones is the one that closes the third inner BIBL. So it seems the outer BIBL is never closed, or it is closed by that same tag? That would be overlapping which is not allowed.

Therefore, the original XML is malformed. We need to either remove the outer BIBL entirely and make each work a separate BIBL, or keep the outer BIBL and move the inner ones inside as children with a different structure. Since we are told to add XML-tags where faulty if it makes sense, I think the simplest is to flatten the structure: each book entry (including the sub-entries) becomes a top-level BIBL. That will result in many BIBL elements, but it is well-formed.

So, we will create a list of BIBL elements:

- BIBL1: Author Justi Lipsii, Title Epistolarum Centuriae complet, Format 4to, Prize 1 Gulden 36 xr.
- BIBL2: Author Ejusd, Title Politicorum Libri? But then the sub-entries: we should treat the sub-entries as separate. However, "Politicorum Libri" might be a main title, and the sub-entries are parts. But we don't have enough info. I think we should treat each inner BIBL as a separate BIBL, and ignore the outer "Politicorum Libri" because it has no prize or format. But then we lose the main title. Alternatively, we could include the main title as a separate BIBL with no prize? But that seems odd.

Looking at the original, after "Ejusd. Politicorum Libri." there is a dot, then the inner BIBLs. It could be that the inner BIBLs are separate works by the same author, and "Politicorum Libri" is actually a title that encompasses them? But the inner BIBLs have their own titles. Perhaps the "Politicorum Libri" is a book that contains multiple parts? For example, Monita, De Magnirudine Romana, De Constantia are separate treatises? Actually, Lipsius wrote multiple works: Politicorum Libri (a work), and also Monita (maybe a separate short work), etc. But in the auction list, they are likely separate lots. The nested structure might be a mistake in the OCR.

Given the instruction "Add xml-tags if faulty where it makes sense", I think we should restructure so that each BIBL corresponds to a single lot. The inner BIBLs likely are independent lots. Therefore, we should not have a BIBL for "Politicorum Libri" that encloses others. Instead, we should have:

- BIBL with AUTHOR Ejusd, TITLE Politicorum Libri? But that would have no prize. However, there is a prize later for De Constantia, not for the main. So maybe the main is not a separate item. I think we should omit the outer "Politicorum Libri" as a separate BIBL and only include the inner ones. But then we need to keep the author for each inner one. So:

BIBL: AUTHOR Ejusd, TITLE Monita, VOLUME 8e Exempla politica? No prize/format.
BIBL: AUTHOR Ejusd, TITLE De Magnirudine Romana.
BIBL: AUTHOR Ejusd, TITLE De Constantia, FORMAT 4to, PRIZE fl. 1. xr. 36.

Similarly, for the next one: BIBL: AUTHOR Ejusd, TITLE de Militia Romana cum An. fig., FORMAT 4to, PRIZE fl. 1. xr. 30.

And so on.

Also note that in the inner BIBL for De Constantia, it has "ibid." and a symbol, which we keep.

Now, also there is a BIBL with <PRICE> instead of <PRIZE> for the "Dissert. apud Principes" entry. We'll change that to <PRIZE>.

Also there are some BIBL with missing author like "Delicia Urbis Bernae" and "Paragone della Pobsia" etc.

Now, let's enumerate all BIBL elements in order after flattening. I'll go through the string and list each.

I'll write a pseudo list:

1. Justi Lipsii, Epistolarum Centuriae complet, 4to, prize 1 Gulden 36 xr.
2. (Ejusd) - but the next is Politicorum Libri. But inside that outer we have:
   a. Ejusd, Monita, volume 8e Exempla politica (no prize)
   b. Ejusd, De Magnirudine Romana (no prize)
   c. Ejusd, De Constantia, 4to, prize fl.1.36
   We will treat a,b,c as BIBL entries 2,3,4. Skip the "Politicorum Libri" line as it seems to be a header.
3. Ejusd, de Militia Romana cum An. fig., 4to, prize fl.1.30
4. Ejusd, Poliorecticon de Machinis..., 4to, prize fl.1.12
5. Ejusd, de Amphitheatro, and then inner BIBLs for de Amphitheatris extra Romam? Actually: <BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>de Amphitheatro</TITLE>. <BIBL><TITLE>de Amphitheatris extra Romam, cum An.fig.</TITLE> <FORMAT>4to</FORMAT>-ibid. <PRIZE>30. xr.</PRIZE></BIBL> So again nested: outer "de Amphitheatro" and inner "de Amphitheatris extra Romam". Similar situation. We should flatten: keep outer? The outer has no prize, so perhaps again it's a header. So treat inner as separate. So entries:
   a. Ejusd, de Amphitheatro? Or skip? I think skip outer and take inner.
   So: Ejusd, de Amphitheatris extra Romam, 4to, prize 30 xr.
